Class Player

  • java.lang.Object
    • Player

  • public class Player
extends java.lang.Object
    Represents one player in a game of Egyptian Rat Screw with their position in turn order and current deck.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Player(int position, java.lang.String pattern)
      Creates a new Player object with an empty deck.
      Player(int position, java.lang.String pattern, java.util.LinkedList<Card> startingDeck)
      Creates a new Player object.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addCards(java.util.LinkedList<Card> cards)
      Appends to the bottom of this Player's deck all of the Cards in order.
      void createDeck(java.util.LinkedList<Card> deck)
      Instantiates this Player's deck if the list is currently empty.
      static int findPlayerByNum(java.util.LinkedList<Player> players, int playerNum)
      Searches a LinkedList of Players for the Player with the specified position.
      java.util.LinkedList<Card> getDeck()
      Get the set of Cards that this player is currently holding.
      java.lang.String getPattern()
      Get the slapping pattern that this Player is watching for.
      int getPlayerNum()
      Gets the position of this Player in the turn order.
      Card playCard()
      Removes and returns the top Card from this Player's deck.
      boolean slaps(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
      Determines whether or not the Player should slap this pile based on the pattern they are watching for.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Get this Player in String format.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • Player

        public Player(int position,
              java.lang.String pattern)
        Creates a new Player object with an empty deck.
        Parameters:
        position - position in the turn order
        pattern - slapping pattern to watch for

      • Player

        public Player(int position,
              java.lang.String pattern,
              java.util.LinkedList<Card> startingDeck)
        Creates a new Player object.
        Parameters:
        position - position in the turn order
        pattern - slapping pattern to watch for
        startingDeck -

    • Method Detail

      • getPlayerNum

        public int getPlayerNum()
        Gets the position of this Player in the turn order.
        Returns:
        the Player's position

      • getDeck

        public java.util.LinkedList<Card> getDeck()
        Get the set of Cards that this player is currently holding.
        Returns:
        the Cards in order

      • getPattern

        public java.lang.String getPattern()
        Get the slapping pattern that this Player is watching for.
        Returns:
        the pattern

      • createDeck

        public void createDeck(java.util.LinkedList<Card> deck)
        Instantiates this Player's deck if the list is currently empty. If the Player's deck already has Cards, does nothing.
        Parameters:
        deck - the set of Cards to assign to this Player

      • addCards

        public void addCards(java.util.LinkedList<Card> cards)
        Appends to the bottom of this Player's deck all of the Cards in order.
        Parameters:
        cards - the Cards to add

      • playCard

        public Card playCard()
        Removes and returns the top Card from this Player's deck.
        Returns:
        the Card

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Get this Player in String format. The String has the Player's position, the pattern they are watching for, and their deck in order.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        the String

      • slaps

        public boolean slaps(java.util.LinkedList<Card> pile)
        Determines whether or not the Player should slap this pile based on the pattern they are watching for.
        Parameters:
        pile - the current pile of Cards in play
        Returns:
        true if the Player should slap, false otherwise

      • findPlayerByNum

        public static int findPlayerByNum(java.util.LinkedList<Player> players,
                                  int playerNum)
        Searches a LinkedList of Players for the Player with the specified position.
        Parameters:
        players - list of Players
        playerNum - the position of the Player to find
        Returns:
        the index in the list of the matching Player, or -1 if there is no matching Player in the list
